step_by_step_thinking:

The data supports that even in ideal cases where there are no complicating factors, the amount of bone loss typically does not exceed 0.1 mm annually once stabilization occurs. This low level of resorption indicates good integration and stability of the implant into the jawbone tissue.

**Final Answer:** B: Less than 0.1 mm. Based on our prior discussions and available scientific literature, this accurately reflects the anticipated annual bone loss following proper healing and stabilization of a functional dental implant.
